channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Build 2026: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework
item.description: 9 min read · June 3, 2026 · Sebastian Kohlmeier Shipping an AI agent is the easy part. Keeping it accurate, safe, and accountable in production is where teams get stuck. Agents are non-deterministic. Their behavior shifts as models update, tools change, and traffic patterns evolve and most of that...
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Kohlmeier

item.title: [Launched] General Availability: Backup your ledger files for audit with Azure confidential ledger’s new tool
item.description: Ever wondered how to allow auditing personas (i.e. security analyst in your organization or security assessment contractor) the ability to view your ledger files and establish trust in the cryptographic proofs themselves? With the new localized, web ledg
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Text Analytics for Health NextGen Playground in Azure AI Language
item.description: Azure AI Language brings the next-generation Text Analytics for Health (TA4H) playground to general availability in the Microsoft Foundry portal. Clinical informatics teams and ISVs can paste discharge summaries, doctor's notes, or research abstracts and
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: User feedback logging in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: This feature introduces end user feedback logging semantics that enables developers to capture structured feedback signals from real users interacting with AI agents—such as thumbs up/down, ratings, or custom annotations—and automatically associates that
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Self-serve custom photo avatar creation in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry brings self-serve custom photo avatar creation to GA in the Foundry NextGen fine-tuning portal. Enterprise customers building voice agents with avatars or producing content with avatar and speech often need a branded avatar instead of a
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: GA: DCasv6 and ECasv6 confidential VMs based on 4th Generation AMD EPYC™ processors
item.description: Today, Azure has expanded its confidential computing offerings with the general availability of the DCasv6 and ECasv6 confidential VMs. Regional availability June 03 2026: Australia Central, Australia Central 2, Australia Southeast, Austria East, Belgium Central, Brazil South, Brazil Southeast,...
item.dc:creator: Rakeshginjupalli

item.title: Build Enterprise-Ready Agents with Microsoft IQ and Oracle AI Database@Azure — now with Oracle MCP
item.description: We’re making it easier to bring Oracle data into intelligent applications with Microsoft IQ and Oracle AI Database@Azure. Oracle Managed Database MCP (Model Context Protocol) remote servers are now available in Microsoft Foundry—introducing a fully managed option that simplifies AI integration...
item.dc:creator: RamKakani

item.title: DevOps for Microsoft Hosted Agents: From Terraform Apply to Production-Grade Agent Delivery
item.description: A companion piece to Infrastructure as Code for AI: Building and Deploying Microsoft Hosted Agents with Terraform. Just announced — source-code deploy (preview). Foundry has just added a second Hosted Agent deploy path alongside the container path this post covers. Instead of a container image, you...
item.dc:creator: LZhang

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift in Belgium Central
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) is now generally available in Azure Belgium Central, expanding regional availability for OpenShift deployments across Europe. With the launch of Microsoft’s first Azure region in Belgium, customers can now run fully managed O
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Agent-to-agent (A2A) support for Prompt agents and Hosted agents in Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry Agent Service adds agent-to-agent (A2A) communication in public preview for Prompt agents and Hosted agents that use the responses protocol. Agents can call other agents by name through a managed endpoint, with identity, content safety,
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: What’s new in Observability at Build 2026
item.description: Observability for AI and Agent Workloads AI agents are moving from prototype to production. To help teams ship and operate agents with the same rigor as the rest of their stack, Azure Monitor & Azure Copilot Observability agent brings end-to-end agent observability — grounded in OpenTelemetry so...
item.dc:creator: Priyanka Nanda

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Conversational PII NextGen Playground in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: In public preview, Microsoft Foundry now offers a Conversational PII (ConvPII) NextGen Playground with transcript input and an API Configuration Panel, enabling developers to validate PII detection across multi-speaker dialogue before integrating the API.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: TextPII NextGen Playground updates in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: In public preview, Microsoft Foundry now offers an updated TextPII NextGen Playground with a refreshed API Configuration Panel, including preview features introduced at Ignite 2025. This update lets developers test predefined PII categories, redaction mod
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Observability developer experience in Azure Developer CLI (azd)
item.description: Use the Azure Developer CLI (azd) CLI evaluation experience to add a measured quality loop to an agent created with Microsoft Foundry. It empowers users on the hosted-agent lifecycle in azd, where you create, provision, deploy, initialize evaluation asset
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Domain filter for specialized model discovery in Foundry model catalog
item.description: Microsoft Foundry model catalog adds a domain filter in public preview, helping developers narrow the catalog of over 1,900 models to the ones trained for a specific industry or use case. Filters cover domains such as robotics, biomedical sciences, materi
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Foundry for Visual Studio Code (June Build 2026 refresh)
item.description: The Microsoft Foundry extension for Visual Studio Code reaches general availability with a Build 2026 refresh that brings the full model catalog, model playground, and Hosted agent deployment into the editor. Developers can sign in with their Azure accoun
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Evaluations with Intelligent Trace Sampling
item.description: Microsoft Foundry observability introduces intelligent trace filtering and sampling for evaluations in public preview. Instead of running evaluations against all production traces, Foundry now select a representative subset of traces using a multi-stage M
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Custom Voice portal experience in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Azure AI Speech moves the Custom Voice authoring experience into the Microsoft Foundry portal in general availability. Approved Custom Voice customers can now upload voice talent recordings and consent statements, run data quality checks, train neural voi
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Rubric evaluator in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry introduces rubric evaluators for measuring agent quality, now in public preview for single-turn and multi-turn agent flows. Rubric evaluators enable developers to define context-specific evaluation criteria using agent prompts, productio
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: How Microsoft is migrating repositories to GitHub
item.description: For the past decade, Azure DevOps has powered software development at Microsoft, supporting some of our largest repositories and most complex engineering workflows across Azure Repos, Boards, and Pipelines. Software development is being reshaped by AI, and where code lives now have a direct impact...
item.dc:creator: Poonam Gupta

item.title: Announcing Confidential Live Migration in Azure
item.description: Authored by Caroline Perez-Vargas, Michael McReynolds Introduction Today at Build, Microsoft showcased Confidential Live Migration, a new capability that helps move Intel® TDX Confidential VMs to updated infrastructure with limited interruption while helping protect VM memory and execution context...
item.dc:creator: mmcrey

item.title: [In development] Announcing: Confidential Live Migration for Intel TDX confidential VMs in Azure
item.description: Microsoft is announcing Confidential Live Migration in Azure, a new capability designed to improve operational flexibility and availability for Intel® TDX Confidential VMs during platform servicing. Confidential Live Migration helps move a Confidential VM
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Event Grid: Powering IoT and Event-Driven Applications at Scale
item.description: Modern IoT and event-driven applications demand more than reliable messaging. They require the ability to handle larger payloads, efficiently route data at scale, and automatically adapt to changing workloads. Today, we're excited to introduce a new set of capabilities in Azure Event Grid Namespace...
item.dc:creator: Connected-Seth

item.title: Is 94% of your syslog just noise? Now you can filter it out before ingestion.
item.description: At Microsoft Build 2026, we are announcing the public preview of multi-stage transformations for Azure Monitor Data Collection Rules (DCRs). Multi-stage transformations let you filter, aggregate, parse, and map your logs at the point of collection, before data is ingested into your workspace....
item.dc:creator: Ivan_Varnitski

item.title: When Telemetry Volume Gets Real: Azure Monitor pipeline’s Performance Story!
item.description: What is Azure Monitor pipeline? Azure Monitor pipeline provides centralized governance and a single point of control that runs close to your data sources, so you can filter, transform, aggregate, and route telemetry before it's sent to Azure Monitor. This approach helps you reduce ingestion volume,...
item.dc:creator: susaraswat4

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Infrastructure Resiliency Manager
item.description: Azure Infrastructure Resiliency Manager is now in public preview, offering a unified, goal-driven experience to design, assess, and improve application resiliency across Azure. It brings together capabilities across Availability Zones, Azure Advisor, Chao
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Any source. Any destination. Ready for AI-era.
item.description: Telemetry is exploding, every new app, edge node, and AI agent is a new firehose, and AI has raised the bar on what that telemetry must be: governed, on open standards, observable at agent scale. Today, most teams answer that by stitching together a stack of disconnected tools, each catering to a...
item.dc:creator: shayoni_seth

item.title: Introducing the New Browser Automation Tool with Toolboxes in Foundry
item.description: AI agents can reason through complex problems—but they hit a wall when workflows require clicking buttons, filling forms, or navigating web interfaces. The Browser Automation Tool bridges that gap, giving your agents the ability to interact with any website the same way a human would. Here's what's...
item.dc:creator: NandiniMuralidharan

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL - Flexible Server: DuckDB extension
item.description: You can now install the DuckDB extension in Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server. Learn more.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Advanced full-text search in Azure DocumentDB
item.description: Now you can unify your search operations in a single database. Azure DocumentDB has added fuzzy search, proximity search, more language support, and BM25 ranking to its full-text capabilities, bringing vector and advanced text search operations together.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Expanding the Reach of Document Translation – New Capabilities Announced at Microsoft Build
item.description: Learn how new Document Translation capabilities in Azure Translator, available in Foundry Tools, help developers translate images, PDFs, Office files, DITA, XLIFF, and future LLM-powered document workflows. The post Expanding the Reach of Document Translation – New Capabilities Announced at...
item.dc:creator: Swetha Machanavajhala

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.123
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.123 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Azure Speech at Build 2026: Powering Voice Agents with Real-Time and Life-like Experiences
item.description: Voice is rapidly becoming the default interface for AI. At Build 2026, Azure Speech in Foundry Tools is making it dramatically easier to ship production-grade voice experiences that feel real, responsive, and global. From agentic, low-latency Voice Live experiences built natively into Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: DONGLI

item.title: Your first model deployment on Foundry Local on Azure Local: from catalog to inference in 10 minutes
item.description: Foundry Local on Azure Local lets you run open-source models directly on your own Azure Local cluster, behind an OpenAI-compatible AP. It's the same experience you've gotten used to in the cloud, but the inference runs on hardware you own. Foundry Local on Azure Local is in public preview at the...
item.dc:creator: JonathanSchtechel

item.title: Announcing Foundry Managed Compute: Run open models in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Microsoft Foundry Managed Compute is a new GPU platform-as-a-service for hosting open-source and custom AI models behind the same endpoint, SDKs, and bill as frontier models. The post Announcing Foundry Managed Compute: Run open models in Microsoft Foundry appeared first on Microsoft Foundry Blog.
item.dc:creator: Manoj Bableshwar

item.title: Introducing Agent Optimizer in Foundry Agent Service
item.description: With hosted agents, we made it straightforward to build and deploy agents on Foundry. You write your logic, run azd deploy, and your agent is live. But “live” and “production-ready” aren’t the same thing. The gap shows up quickly. Your customer support agent handles requests, but it forgets to ask...
item.dc:creator: Luis Quintanilla, Victor Dibia

item.title: Making agent memory more reliable, transparent, and production-ready
item.description: Memory has always mattered for personalization and continuity. But as customers move agents from demos into production, another requirement becomes just as important: reliability. Enterprise teams need agents that not only remember facts, but also apply what they have learned to follow procedures...
item.dc:creator: Lewis Liu

item.title: Discovery to Execution: Scaling Agents with Toolboxes and Routines in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Tooling doesn’t break at a small scale—it breaks when teams move to production. AI adoption accelerates, so does the number of tools available to them. Discovering, managing and securing the right tools has quickly become one of the biggest challenges in building production-grade agents. Today, we...
item.dc:creator: Linda Li, Maria Naggaga

item.title: From Building Agents to Working with Them: Enterprise Agent Distribution in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: The past year was about building agents. The next year is about putting them to work. Organizations have moved quickly from experimenting with AI agents to building ones that perform complex business processes and execute long-running tasks. But the bottleneck has shifted. The challenge is no...
item.dc:creator: Amanda Foster

item.title: Embed intelligence into physical systems with smaller form factor infrastructure (preview)
item.description: Written by Cosmos Darwin, Azure Edge PM, and Michael MacKenzie, VP of Digital Operations AI is transforming how we work, but so far it's mostly lived on your screen: agents and models assisting with information work. How can that intelligence take on physical work, too? Jobs that happen out in the...
item.dc:creator: Cosmos_Darwin

item.title: Announcing Preview of Guest RDMA for Azure Boost
item.description: We’re excited to announce Guest RDMA (Preview) in Azure, beginning today in our UK South region, bringing high-throughput, ultra-low latency networking directly into guest virtual machines anywhere within the same region. By using Azure Boost to enable RDMA capabilities on our frontend network...
item.dc:creator: MengxiWu

item.title: Azure SRE Agent at Microsoft Build 2026: Bringing agentic operations to the enterprise
item.description: Build 2026 Update When we launched Azure SRE Agent, the promise was simple: reduce operational toil, improve up time, and evolve teams from manual incident response towards AI-powered operations. Since GA in March 2026, that promise has held up in production. Teams are using the agent to diagnose...
item.dc:creator: dchelupati

item.title: 📢 Announcing Knowledge as a Service for Azure Logic Apps
item.description: Now in Public Preview Turn your documents into a ready-to-use knowledge base without custom RAG pipelines. Today at Microsoft Build 2026, we are announcing the Public Preview of Knowledge as a Service for Azure Logic Apps. It is a managed knowledge layer that transforms your documents into a...
item.dc:creator: DivSwa

item.title: Foundry Labs @ Build 2026
item.description: We launched Foundry Labs last year as the home for cutting-edge AI experiments from across Microsoft. It is meant to be a place where developers could try, fork, and build with our earliest prototypes. The response from the community has been tremendous, and we're doubling down. Today at Build...
item.dc:creator: Saumil-Shrivastava

item.title: Evaluate before you ship: introducing the Voice Live Evaluation Harness
item.description: You've built a voice agent on Azure Voice Live. It demos beautifully. Then a teammate asks the question that keeps every voice-agent team up at night: "How do we know it's actually good — across 200 customer calls, not the three we just listened to?" Until today, the honest answer was: put on...
item.dc:creator: SolarRezaei

item.title: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, May releases
item.description: VS Code continues with weekly stable releases. This changelog covers releases v1.120 through v1.123, the releases we shipped throughout May and early June 2026. In May, we made the Agents… The post GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, May releases appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: v1.16.0-alpha20260603
item.description: 1.16.0-alpha20260603 (June 03, 2026) NEW FEATURES: Store PlannedPrivate data for providers (#37986) New store block in terraform_data that can handle ephemeral and sensitive values (#38298) Providers can now use nested blocks as computed values (#38305) import: add support for import blocks inside...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: File share migrations simplified with Azure Copilot Migration Agent
item.description: Building on our earlier announcement of discovery and assessment support for SMB and NFS file shares in Azure Migrate, we are extending the experience to support end-to-end file share migrations within the same workflow. With Azure Copilot Migration Agent, customers can move from discovery and...
item.dc:creator: madhurinrao

item.title: Build, deploy, and govern sovereign AI with Foundry Local on Azure Local
item.description: Not every AI workload can run in the cloud. For many of our customers, data needs to stay within defined boundaries, connectivity may be limited or absent, and latency, governance, and auditability are non-negotiable. With Foundry Local on Azure Local, you can use the same model catalog, developer...
item.dc:creator: insagiv

item.title: Designing Cloud Landing Zones by Traffic Flow: A Defence‑in‑Depth, DMZ‑First Architecture
item.description: As enterprises adopt Microsoft Azure for large‑scale and regulated workloads, security architecture must be driven by traffic trust boundaries and inspection intent, not connectivity alone. Regulatory frameworks consistently require a clear separation between Internet‑untrusted and private...
item.dc:creator: AvanishYadav
